```typescript
import * as vscode from 'vscode';
import * as path from 'path';
import * as fs from 'fs';
import { parseMarkdown } from './markdown-parser';
import { submitGenerate, queryResult, isTransientError } from './api';
import { extFromUrl } from './image-utils';
import { assemblePrompt } from './prompt-injection';
import { readConfig } from './config';
import type { TaskCardInfo } from './shared';

const STORAGE_KEY = 'runningTasks';
const POLL_INTERVAL = 4000;
const TASK_TIMEOUT = 10 * 60 * 1000; // 10 ÂàÜÈíü
const MAX_RETRY = 5;

interface JobState {
  index: number;
  id: string;
  status: 'submitting' | 'running' | 'succeeded' | 'failed' | 'violation';
  progress: number;
  error: string;
  transientRetries: number;
}

interface TaskState {
  folderName: string;
  mdDirectory: string;
  mdBaseName: string;
  model: string;
  startTime: number;
  sessionStartTime: number;
  jobs: JobState[];
  downloadedImages: string[];
}

export class TaskManager {
  private tasks: Map<string, TaskState> = new Map();
  private timer: ReturnType<typeof setInterval> | null = null;
  private polling = false;
  private _onUpdate?: (tasks: TaskCardInfo[], autoSwitch?: boolean) => void;

  constructor(private readonly context: vscode.ExtensionContext) {}

  set onUpdate(cb: (tasks: TaskCardInfo[], autoSwitch?: boolean) => void) { this._onUpdate = cb; }

  /** ÊÅ¢Â§çÊåÅ‰πÖÂåñÁöÑËøõË°å‰∏≠‰ªªÂä° */
  resume() {
    const stored = this.context.globalState.get<TaskState[]>(STORAGE_KEY) ?? [];
    const now = Date.now();
    for (const task of stored) {
      task.sessionStartTime = now;
      for (const job of task.jobs) {
        if (job.status === 'submitting') {
          job.status = 'failed';
          job.error = 'Êèê‰∫§Êú™ÂÆåÊàêÔºàÊâ©Â±ïÂ∑≤ÂÖ≥Èó≠Ôºâ';
        }
      }
      this.tasks.set(task.folderName, task);
    }
    this.emitUpdate();
    if (this.tasks.size > 0) {
      this.startPolling();
    }
  }

  private persist() {
    const arr = Array.from(this.tasks.values());
    void this.context.globalState.update(STORAGE_KEY, arr);
  }

  /** Êèê‰∫§Êñ∞‰ªªÂä° */
  async submit(mdUri: vscode.Uri): Promise<string> {
    const config = await readConfig(this.context);
    if (!config.apiKey) {
      throw new Error('ËØ∑ÂÖàËÆæÁΩÆ API Key');
    }

    const raw = fs.readFileSync(mdUri.fsPath, 'utf-8');
    if (raw.trim() === '') {
      throw new Error('ÂÜÖÂÆπ‰∏∫Á©∫');
    }

    const mdDir = path.dirname(mdUri.fsPath);
    const { body, images, errors: parseErrors } = parseMarkdown(raw, mdDir);
    if (parseErrors.length > 0) {
      throw new Error(`ÂèÇËÄÉÂõæËØªÂèñÂ§±Ë¥•:\n${parseErrors.join('\n')}`);
    }

    const finalPrompt = assemblePrompt(config.model, config.modelInjections, body, mdDir);

    const folderName = createTaskFolder(mdDir);

    const now = Date.now();
    const N = Math.min(config.concurrency, 10);
    const jobs: JobState[] = [];
    for (let i = 1; i <= N; i++) {
      jobs.push({
        index: i,
        id: '',
        status: 'submitting',
        progress: 0,
        error: '',
        transientRetries: 0,
      });
    }

    const task: TaskState = {
      folderName,
      mdDirectory: mdDir,
      mdBaseName: path.basename(mdUri.fsPath, '.md'),
      model: config.model,
      startTime: now,
      sessionStartTime: now,
      jobs,
      downloadedImages: [],
    };

    // Á´ãÂç≥Âª∫Âç°ËøîÂõûÔºåautoSwitch ‰øÉ‰ΩøÂâçÁ´ØÂàáÂà∞‰ªªÂä°È°µ
    this.tasks.set(folderName, task);
    this.persist();
    this.emitUpdate(true);
    this.startPolling();

    // ÂêéÂè∞Âπ∂ÂèëÂèëÂá∫ N ‰∏™ generate ËØ∑Ê±Ç
    const refImages = images.map(i => i.dataUri);
    for (const job of jobs) {
      void this.submitJob(task, job, finalPrompt, refImages, config);
    }

    return folderName;
  }

  private async submitJob(
    task: TaskState,
    job: JobState,
    prompt: string,
    refImages: string[],
    config: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof readConfig>>
  ): Promise<void> {
    try {
      const jobId = await submitGenerate({
        baseUrl: config.baseUrl,
        apiKey: config.apiKey,
        model: config.model,
        prompt,
        images: refImages,
        aspectRatio: config.aspectRatio,
        imageSize: config.imageSize,
      });
      job.id = jobId;
      job.status = 'running';
      job.transientRetries = 0;
    } catch (err) {
      if (isTransientError(err) && job.transientRetries < MAX_RETRY) {
        job.transientRetries++;
        this.persist();
        await this.submitJob(task, job, prompt, refImages, config);
        return;
      }
      job.status = 'failed';
      job.error = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
    }
    this.persist();
    this.emitUpdate();

    // Ê£ÄÊü•ÊòØÂê¶ÂÖ®ÈÉ®Êèê‰∫§Â§±Ë¥•
    const allDone = task.jobs.every(j => j.status !== 'submitting');
    if (allDone) {
      const allFailed = task.jobs.every(j => j.status === 'failed');
      if (allFailed) {
        this.failTask(task);
      }
    }
  }

  private startPolling() {
    if (this.timer) return;
    this.timer = setInterval(() => {
      void this.pollAll();
    }, POLL_INTERVAL);
  }

  private stopPolling() {
    if (this.timer) {
      clearInterval(this.timer);
      this.timer = null;
    }
  }

  private async pollAll() {
    if (this.polling) return;
    this.polling = true;
    try {
      const config = await readConfig(this.context);
      const now = Date.now();

      for (const task of this.tasks.values()) {
        // Ë∂ÖÊó∂ÂÖúÂ∫ï
        if (now - task.sessionStartTime > TASK_TIMEOUT) {
          for (const job of task.jobs) {
            if (job.status === 'running' || job.status === 'submitting') {
              job.status = 'failed';
              job.error = '‰ªªÂä°Ë∂ÖÊó∂Ôºà10 ÂàÜÈíüÔºâ';
            }
          }
          this.finishTask(task);
          continue;
        }

        let allDone = true;
        for (const job of task.jobs) {
          if (job.status === 'running') {
            allDone = false;
            try {
              const result = await queryResult(config.baseUrl, config.apiKey, job.id);
              job.progress = result.progress ?? job.progress;

              if (result.status === 'succeeded' && result.results) {
                const folderPath = path.join(task.mdDirectory, task.folderName);
                for (const r of result.results) {
                  const seq = task.downloadedImages.length + 1;
                  const ext = extFromUrl(r.url);
                  const filename = `${task.mdBaseName}-${seq}.${ext}`;
                  const dest = path.join(folderPath, filename);
                  await downloadFile(r.url, dest);
                  task.downloadedImages.push(filename);
                }
                job.status = 'succeeded';
                job.progress = 100;
                job.transientRetries = 0;
              } else if (result.status === 'failed' || result.status === 'violation') {
                job.status = result.status;
                job.error = result.error ?? 'ÁîüÊàêÂ§±Ë¥•';
              }
            } catch (err) {
              if (isTransientError(err)) {
                allDone = false;
              } else {
                job.status = 'failed';
                job.error = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
              }
            }
          }

          if (job.status === 'submitting') {
            allDone = false;
          }
        }

        if (allDone) {
          this.finishTask(task);
        }
      }
      this.persist();
    } finally {
      this.polling = false;
    }
  }

  /** ‰ªªÂä°ÁªàÁªìÂ§ÑÁêÜ */
  private finishTask(task: TaskState) {
    const successCount = task.jobs.filter(j => j.status === 'succeeded').length;
    const failedJobs = task.jobs.filter(j => j.status === 'failed' || j.status === 'violation');
    const errors = [...new Set(failedJobs.map(j => j.error).filter(Boolean))];

    if (successCount === 0) {
      const folderPath = path.join(task.mdDirectory, task.folderName);
      try {
        if (fs.readdirSync(folderPath).length === 0) {
          fs.rmdirSync(folderPath);
        }
      } catch { /* ÂøΩÁï• */ }

      if (errors.length > 0) {
        vscode.window.showErrorMessage(
          `‰ªªÂä° ${task.folderName} ÂÖ®ÈÉ®Â§±Ë¥•: ${errors.join('; ')}`
        );
      }
    } else if (failedJobs.length > 0) {
      vscode.window.showWarningMessage(
        `‰ªªÂä° ${task.folderName} ÂÆåÊàê ${successCount}/${task.jobs.length}Ôºå${failedJobs.length} ÂºÝÂ§±Ë¥•: ${errors.join('; ')}`
      );
    }

    this.tasks.delete(task.folderName);
    this.persist();
    this.emitUpdate();

    if (this.tasks.size === 0) {
      this.stopPolling();
    }
  }

  /** ÂÖ®ÈÉ®Êèê‰∫§Â§±Ë¥•‚Äî‚ÄîÊï¥‰ªªÂä°‰ΩúÂ∫ü */
  private failTask(task: TaskState) {
    const folderPath = path.join(task.mdDirectory, task.folderName);
    try {
      if (fs.readdirSync(folderPath).length === 0) {
        fs.rmdirSync(folderPath);
      }
    } catch { /* ÂøΩÁï• */ }

    const errors = [...new Set(task.jobs.map(j => j.error).filter(Boolean))];
    if (errors.length > 0) {
      vscode.window.showErrorMessage(
        `‰ªªÂä°Êèê‰∫§ÂÖ®ÈÉ®Â§±Ë¥•: ${errors.join('; ')}`
      );
    }

    this.tasks.delete(task.folderName);
    this.persist();
    this.emitUpdate();

    if (this.tasks.size === 0) {
      this.stopPolling();
    }
  }

  /** ËÆ°ÁÆóËÅöÂêàËøõÂ∫¶ */
  private calcProgress(task: TaskState): number {
    const N = task.jobs.length;
    if (N === 0) return 0;
    let total = 0;
    for (const job of task.jobs) {
      if (job.status === 'succeeded' || job.status === 'failed' || job.status === 'violation') {
        total += 100;
      } else if (job.status === 'running') {
        total += job.progress;
      }
    }
    return Math.round(total / N);
  }

  /** ÂèëÂ∞Ñ‰ªªÂä°Âç°ÂàóË°®ÁªôÂâçÁ´Ø */
  private emitUpdate(autoSwitch = false) {
    if (!this._onUpdate) return;
    const cards: TaskCardInfo[] = Array.from(this.tasks.values()).map(t => ({
      folderName: t.folderName,
      model: t.model,
      status: t.jobs.every(j => j.status === 'submitting') ? 'submitting' :
              t.jobs.every(j => j.status === 'succeeded') ? 'done' : 'running',
      jobCount: t.jobs.length,
      doneCount: t.jobs.filter(j => j.status === 'succeeded').length,
      failedCount: t.jobs.filter(j => j.status === 'failed' || j.status === 'violation').length,
      progress: this.calcProgress(t),
      startTime: t.startTime,
      downloadedImages: t.downloadedImages,
      errors: t.jobs.map(j => j.error).filter(Boolean),
      isActive: true,
    }));
    cards.sort((a, b) => b.folderName.localeCompare(a.folderName));
    this._onUpdate(cards, autoSwitch);
  }

  dispose() {
    this.stopPolling();
  }
}

// --- Â∑•ÂÖ∑ÂáΩÊï∞ ---

function pad(n: number): string { return String(n).padStart(2, '0'); }

function createTaskFolder(mdDir: string): string {
  const now = new Date();
  const ts = `${String(now.getFullYear()).slice(2)}${pad(now.getMonth() + 1)}${pad(now.getDate())}${pad(now.getHours())}${pad(now.getMinutes())}${pad(now.getSeconds())}`;
  let seq = 0;
  let name: string;
  do {
    seq++;
    name = `task-${ts}-${seq}`;
  } while (fs.existsSync(path.join(mdDir, name)));
  fs.mkdirSync(path.join(mdDir, name));
  return name;
}

async function downloadFile(url: string, dest: string): Promise<void> {
  const res = await fetch(url);
  if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`‰∏ãËΩΩÂ§±Ë¥•: ${res.status}`);
  const buf = Buffer.from(await res.arrayBuffer());
  fs.writeFileSync(dest, buf);
}

<details>
<summary><code>/home/particle/2026-1/harness-human-research/image-flow/runs/eval-manual-superpowers-260611093413/src/sidebar-provider.ts</code></summary>

```typescript
import * as vscode from 'vscode';
import * as path from 'path';
import * as fs from 'fs';
import { readConfig, writeConfigField } from './config';
import { scanFolder, autoAssetFolders, getManualFolders, addManualFolder, removeManualFolder, imageRelativeRef } from './asset-library';
import { isImageExt } from './image-utils';
import type { FrontendMessage, InitData, BackendMessage, TaskCardInfo, AssetFolderInfo } from './shared';
import type { TaskManager } from './task-manager';

export class SidebarProvider implements vscode.WebviewViewProvider {
  private _view?: vscode.WebviewView;
  private _effectiveMd = '';
  private _extensionUri: vscode.Uri;
  private _currentTasks: TaskCardInfo[] = [];
  private _editorListener?: vscode.Disposable;

  constructor(
    private readonly context: vscode.ExtensionContext,
    taskManager: TaskManager
  ) {
    this._extensionUri = context.extensionUri;
    taskManager.onUpdate = (tasks, autoSwitch) => {
      this._currentTasks = tasks;
      if (this._view) {
        this.postMessage(this._view.webview, {
          type: 'taskUpdate',
          tasks,
          autoSwitch,
          history: this.scanHistory(),
        });
      }
    };
  }

  resolveWebviewView(webviewView: vscode.WebviewView) {
    this._view = webviewView;

    webviewView.webview.options = {
      enableScripts: true,
      localResourceRoots: this.buildLocalResourceRoots(),
    };

    webviewView.webview.html = this.getHtml(webviewView.webview);

    webviewView.webview.onDidReceiveMessage(async (msg: FrontendMessage) => {
      await this.handleMessage(msg, webviewView.webview);
    });

    // ÁîüÊïà MD Ë∑üÈöèÊ¥ªÂä®ÁºñËæëÂô®
    this._editorListener = vscode.window.onDidChangeActiveTextEditor(editor => {
      if (editor && editor.document.uri.fsPath.endsWith('.md')) {
        this._effectiveMd = editor.document.uri.fsPath;
        this.postMessage(this._view!.webview, { type: 'effectiveMdChange', path: this._effectiveMd });
        this.sendAssetUpdate(this._view!.webview);
      }
      // Èùû MD ‰∏çÊ∏ÖÁ©∫Ôºå‰øùÁïô‰∏ä‰∏Ä‰∏™
    });
  }

  private buildLocalResourceRoots(): vscode.Uri[] {
    const roots = [
      vscode.Uri.joinPath(this._extensionUri, 'media'),
    ];
    const workspaceFolders = vscode.workspace.workspaceFolders;
    if (workspaceFolders) {
      for (const folder of workspaceFolders) {
        roots.push(folder.uri);
      }
    }
    return roots;
  }

  private getHtml(webview: vscode.Webview): string {
    const nonce = getNonce();
    const scriptUri = webview.asWebviewUri(
      vscode.Uri.joinPath(this._extensionUri, 'media', 'sidebar.js')
    );
    const styleUri = webview.asWebviewUri(
      vscode.Uri.joinPath(this._extensionUri, 'media', 'sidebar.css')
    );

    return `<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="zh-CN">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta http-equiv="Content-Security-Policy"
        content="default-src 'none';
                 img-src ${webview.cspSource} data: https:;
                 style-src ${webview.cspSource} 'unsafe-inline';
                 script-src 'nonce-${nonce}';">
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="${styleUri.toString()}">
</head>
<body>
  <div id="root"></div>
  <script nonce="${nonce}" src="${scriptUri.toString()}"></script>
</body>
</html>`;
  }

  private async handleMessage(msg: FrontendMessage, webview: vscode.Webview) {
    switch (msg.type) {
      case 'init': {
        await this.sendInitData(webview);
        break;
      }
      case 'setConfig': {
        await writeConfigField(this.context, msg.key, msg.value);
        const config = await readConfig(this.context);
        this.postMessage(webview, { type: 'configUpdate', config });
        break;
      }
      case 'setApiKey': {
        await this.context.secrets.store('imageFlow.apiKey', msg.key);
        break;
      }
      case 'addAssetFolder': {
        await addManualFolder(this.context);
        this.sendAssetUpdate(webview);
        break;
      }
      case 'removeAssetFolder': {
        await removeManualFolder(this.context, msg.folderPath);
        this.sendAssetUpdate(webview);
        break;
      }
      case 'insertReference': {
        void this.handleInsertReference(msg.imagePath);
        break;
      }
      case 'openInBrowser': {
        vscode.env.openExternal(vscode.Uri.parse(msg.url));
        break;
      }
      default:
        break;
    }
  }

  private async handleInsertReference(imagePath: string) {
    const editor = vscode.window.activeTextEditor;
    if (!editor) return;

    const docUri = editor.document.uri;
    if (!docUri.fsPath.endsWith('.md')) return;

    const mdDir = path.dirname(docUri.fsPath);
    const ref = imageRelativeRef(imagePath, mdDir);
    if (!ref) return;

    await editor.edit(editBuilder => {
      editBuilder.insert(editor.selection.active, ref);
    });
  }

  private buildAssetFolders(webview: vscode.Webview): AssetFolderInfo[] {
    const manualFolders = getManualFolders(this.context);

    let autoFolderPaths: string[] = [];
    if (this._effectiveMd) {
      const workspaceFolder = vscode.workspace.getWorkspaceFolder(
        vscode.Uri.file(this._effectiveMd)
      );
      if (workspaceFolder) {
        autoFolderPaths = autoAssetFolders(this._effectiveMd, workspaceFolder.uri.fsPath);
      }
    }

    const allFolders: { folderPath: string; canRemove: boolean }[] = [
      ...manualFolders.map(f => ({ folderPath: f, canRemove: true })),
      ...autoFolderPaths.map(f => ({ folderPath: f, canRemove: false })),
    ];

    return allFolders.map(({ folderPath, canRemove }) => {
      const images = scanFolder(folderPath).map(p => ({
        url: webview.asWebviewUri(vscode.Uri.file(p)).toString(),
        path: p,
        name: path.basename(p),
      }));
      return {
        name: path.basename(folderPath),
        folderPath,
        images,
        canRemove,
      };
    });
  }

  private sendAssetUpdate(webview: vscode.Webview) {
    const folders = this.buildAssetFolders(webview);
    this.postMessage(webview, { type: 'assetUpdate', folders });
  }

  private async sendInitData(webview: vscode.Webview) {
    const config = await readConfig(this.context);
    const data: InitData = {
      config: { ...config, apiKey: config.apiKey ? '‚Ä¢‚Ä¢‚Ä¢‚Ä¢‚Ä¢‚Ä¢‚Ä¢‚Ä¢' : '' },
      effectiveMd: this._effectiveMd,
      tasks: this._currentTasks,
      assetFolders: [],
    };
    this.postMessage(webview, { type: 'initData', data });
    this.sendAssetUpdate(webview);
  }

  private postMessage(webview: vscode.Webview, msg: BackendMessage) {
    webview.postMessage(msg);
  }

  /** Êâ´Êèè MD ÂêåÁ∫ßÁõÆÂΩï‰∏ãÂéÜÂè≤ task-* Êñá‰ª∂Â§πÔºåÊéíÈô§ËøõË°å‰∏≠ÁöÑ‰ªªÂä° */
  private scanHistory(): TaskCardInfo[] {
    if (!this._effectiveMd) return [];
    const mdDir = path.dirname(this._effectiveMd);
    const excludeFolders = new Set(this._currentTasks.map(t => t.folderName));
    const history: TaskCardInfo[] = [];

    let entries: fs.Dirent[];
    try {
      entries = fs.readdirSync(mdDir, { withFileTypes: true });
    } catch {
      return [];
    }

    for (const entry of entries) {
      if (!entry.isDirectory() || !entry.name.startsWith('task-')) continue;
      if (excludeFolders.has(entry.name)) continue;

      const taskDir = path.join(mdDir, entry.name);
      let imageFiles: string[];
      try {
        imageFiles = fs.readdirSync(taskDir).filter(f => isImageExt(path.extname(f)));
      } catch {
        continue;
      }
      if (imageFiles.length === 0) continue;

      history.push({
        folderName: entry.name,
        model: '',
        status: 'done',
        jobCount: imageFiles.length,
        doneCount: imageFiles.length,
        failedCount: 0,
        progress: 100,
        startTime: 0,
        downloadedImages: imageFiles.map(f => path.join(taskDir, f)),
        errors: [],
        isActive: false,
      });
    }

    history.sort((a, b) => b.folderName.localeCompare(a.folderName));
    return history;
  }

  /** Â§ñÈÉ®ËÆæÁΩÆÁîüÊïà MD */
  setEffectiveMd(mdPath: string) {
    this._effectiveMd = mdPath;
    if (this._view) {
      this.postMessage(this._view.webview, { type: 'effectiveMdChange', path: mdPath });
      this.sendAssetUpdate(this._view.webview);
    }
  }

  /** Â§ñÈÉ®Êé®ÈÄÅ‰ªªÂä°Êõ¥Êñ∞ */
  setTasks(tasks: TaskCardInfo[]) {
    this._currentTasks = tasks;
    if (this._view) {
      this.postMessage(this._view.webview, {
        type: 'taskUpdate',
        tasks,
        history: this.scanHistory(),
      });
    }
  }

  dispose() {
    this._editorListener?.dispose();
  }
}

function getNonce(): string {
  let text = '';
  const possible = 'ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789';
  for (let i = 0; i < 64; i++) {
    text += possible.charAt(Math.floor(Math.random() * possible.length));
  }
  return text;
}

```typescript
import * as path from 'path';
import { readFileSync } from 'fs';
import { extToMime } from './image-utils';

interface RefImage {
  index: number;
  path: string;
  dataUri: string;
}

/** ÂåπÈÖç ![alt](<path>) Êàñ ![alt](path)ÔºåÂ∞ñÊã¨Âè∑ÂÜÖÂèØÂê´Á©∫ÊÝº‰∏éÂçäËßíÊã¨Âè∑ */
const IMAGE_RE = /!\[([^\]]*)\]\((<[^>]+>|[^)\s]+)\)/g;

export function parseMarkdown(
  raw: string,
  mdDir: string
): { body: string; images: RefImage[]; errors: string[] } {
  const errors: string[] = [];
  const urlMap = new Map<string, number>();
  const images: RefImage[] = [];
  let nextIdx = 1;

  const imageTitle = (p: string) => {
    const basename = path.basename(p);
    const dotIdx = basename.lastIndexOf('.');
    return dotIdx > 0 ? basename.slice(0, dotIdx) : basename;
  };

  const body = raw.replace(IMAGE_RE, (_match, _alt, p: string) => {
    // ÂéªÈô§Â∞ñÊã¨Âè∑ÂåÖË£π
    const cleanPath = p.startsWith('<') && p.endsWith('>') ? p.slice(1, -1) : p;

    if (!urlMap.has(cleanPath)) {
      const fullPath = path.resolve(mdDir, cleanPath);
      try {
        const buf = readFileSync(fullPath);
        const ext = path.extname(fullPath).toLowerCase();
        const mime = extToMime(ext);
        const b64 = buf.toString('base64');
        const dataUri = `data:${mime};base64,${b64}`;
        urlMap.set(cleanPath, nextIdx);
        images.push({ index: nextIdx, path: cleanPath, dataUri });
        nextIdx++;
      } catch {
        errors.push(fullPath);
        return _match; // ‰øùÁïôÂéüÊñáÔºåÁ≠âÊúÄÂêé‰∏ÄËµ∑Êä•Èîô
      }
    }

    const idx = urlMap.get(cleanPath)!;
    return `[image${idx}](${imageTitle(cleanPath)})`;
  });

  return { body, images, errors };
}

```typescript
import * as path from 'path';
import * as fs from 'fs';
import * as vscode from 'vscode';

/**
 * ÊãºË£ÖÊúÄÁªà prompt:
 * ÊúÄÁªà prompt = Ê®°ÂûãÊ≥®ÂÖ•Âè• + Â∑•‰ΩúÂå∫ÊÝπ IMAGES.md + ÊõøÊç¢ÂêéÊ≠£Êñá
 * Á©∫Ë°åËøûÊé•ÔºåÁ©∫ÊÆµÁúÅÁï•
 */
export function assemblePrompt(
  model: string,
  injections: Record<string, string>,
  body: string,
  mdDir: string
): string {
  const parts: string[] = [];

  // Ê®°ÂûãÊ≥®ÂÖ•Âè•
  const injection = injections[model];
  if (injection) parts.push(injection);

  // IMAGES.md
  const workspaceFolder = vscode.workspace.getWorkspaceFolder(
    vscode.Uri.file(mdDir)
  );
  if (workspaceFolder) {
    const imagesPath = path.join(workspaceFolder.uri.fsPath, 'IMAGES.md');
    try {
      const content = fs.readFileSync(imagesPath, 'utf-8').trim();
      if (content) parts.push(content);
    } catch { /* ‰∏çÂ≠òÂú®ÔºåÈùôÈªòË∑≥Ëøá */ }
  }

  // Ê≠£Êñá
  if (body) parts.push(body);

  return parts.join('\n\n');
}

````typescript
import * as vscode from 'vscode';

/**
 * ÁîüÊàêÈ¢ÑËßàÊñáÊ°£ÂÜÖÂÆπÔºåÂõõÊÆµÔºö
 * 1. ÊúÄÁªàÊèêÁ§∫ËØç
 * 2. ËØ∑Ê±ÇÂú∞ÂùÄ
 * 3. ËØ∑Ê±ÇÂèÇÊï∞ JSONÔºàÂâîÈô§ prompt ‰∏é imagesÔºâ
 * 4. ÂèÇËÄÉÂõæÊ¶ÇËßà
 */
export function buildPreviewContent(
  finalPrompt: string,
  baseUrl: string,
  body: Record<string, unknown>,
  imageDataUris: string[]
): string {
  const lines: string[] = [];

  lines.push('# È¢ÑËßàËØ∑Ê±Ç');
  lines.push('');

  // 1. ÊúÄÁªàÊèêÁ§∫ËØç
  lines.push('## ÊúÄÁªàÊèêÁ§∫ËØç');
  lines.push('');
  lines.push(finalPrompt);
  lines.push('');

  // 2. ËØ∑Ê±ÇÂú∞ÂùÄ
  lines.push('## ËØ∑Ê±ÇÂú∞ÂùÄ');
  lines.push('');
  lines.push(`POST ${baseUrl}/v1/api/generate`);
  lines.push('');

  // 3. ËØ∑Ê±ÇÂèÇÊï∞ JSONÔºàÂâîÈô§ prompt ‰∏é imagesÔºâ
  const safeBody = { ...body };
  delete safeBody.prompt;
  delete safeBody.images;
  lines.push('## ËØ∑Ê±ÇÂèÇÊï∞');
  lines.push('');
  lines.push('```json');
  lines.push(JSON.stringify(safeBody, null, 2));
  lines.push('```');
  lines.push('');

  // 4. ÂèÇËÄÉÂõæÊ¶ÇËßà
  lines.push('## ÂèÇËÄÉÂõæÊ¶ÇËßà');
  lines.push('');
  if (imageDataUris.length === 0) {
    lines.push('ÊóÝÂèÇËÄÉÂõæ');
  } else {
    for (let i = 0; i < imageDataUris.length; i++) {
      const uri = imageDataUris[i];
      const preview = uri.slice(0, 48);
      lines.push(`- \`image${i + 1}\`: \`${preview}‚Ä¶\`ÔºàÊÄªÈïøÂ∫¶ ${uri.length}Ôºâ`);
    }
  }

  return lines.join('\n');
}

/**
 * ÊâìÂºÄÈ¢ÑËßàÊñáÊ°£Ôºà‰∏çË∞É APIÔºâ
 */
export async function showPreview(
  finalPrompt: string,
  baseUrl: string,
  body: Record<string, unknown>,
  imageDataUris: string[]
) {
  const content = buildPreviewContent(
    finalPrompt,
    baseUrl,
    body,
    imageDataUris
  );

  const doc = await vscode.workspace.openTextDocument({
    content,
    language: 'markdown',
  });

  await vscode.window.showTextDocument(doc, { preview: false });
}

```typescript
import * as vscode from 'vscode';
import * as path from 'path';
import * as fs from 'fs';
import { isImageExt } from './image-utils';

const MAX_DEPTH = 3;
const MAX_ENTRIES = 500;

/** ÈÄíÂΩíÊâ´ÊèèÂõæÁâáÔºà‰∏äÈôê3Â±Ç„ÄÅ500Êù°ÁõÆÔºâ */
export function scanFolder(folderPath: string): string[] {
  const result: string[] = [];
  const stack: { dir: string; depth: number }[] = [{ dir: folderPath, depth: 0 }];

  while (stack.length > 0 && result.length < MAX_ENTRIES) {
    const { dir, depth } = stack.pop()!;
    if (depth > MAX_DEPTH) continue;

    let entries: fs.Dirent[];
    try {
      entries = fs.readdirSync(dir, { withFileTypes: true });
    } catch {
      continue;
    }

    for (const entry of entries) {
      if (result.length >= MAX_ENTRIES) break;
      const fullPath = path.join(dir, entry.name);

      if (entry.isDirectory() && depth < MAX_DEPTH) {
        stack.push({ dir: fullPath, depth: depth + 1 });
      } else if (entry.isFile() && isImageExt(path.extname(entry.name))) {
        result.push(fullPath);
      }
    }
  }

  return result;
}

/** ÊåâÁîüÊïà MD Ë∑ØÂæÑÁîüÊàêËá™Âä®Á¥ÝÊùêÂ∫ìÔºàÈÄêÂ±ÇÔºåÂ∑•‰ΩúÂå∫ÊÝπÁöÑ‰∏ã‰∏ÄÂ±ÇÂà∞ MD ÊâÄÂú®Â±ÇÔºâ */
export function autoAssetFolders(
  mdPath: string,
  workspaceRoot: string
): string[] {
  const rel = path.relative(workspaceRoot, mdPath);
  if (rel.startsWith('..') || path.isAbsolute(rel)) return [];

  const folders: string[] = [];
  let current = path.dirname(mdPath);

  while (current !== workspaceRoot && current !== path.dirname(current)) {
    try {
      const entries = fs.readdirSync(current, { withFileTypes: true });
      const hasImages = entries.some(
        e => e.isFile() && isImageExt(path.extname(e.name))
      );
      if (hasImages) folders.push(current);
    } catch { /* ÂøΩÁï• */ }

    current = path.dirname(current);
    if (current === workspaceRoot || current === path.dirname(workspaceRoot)) break;
  }

  return folders.reverse();
}

/** Ëé∑ÂèñÊâãÂä®Á¥ÝÊùêÂ∫ìÂàóË°® */
export function getManualFolders(context: vscode.ExtensionContext): string[] {
  return context.workspaceState.get<string[]>('assetFolders') ?? [];
}

/** Ê∑ªÂäÝÊâãÂä®Á¥ÝÊùêÂ∫ì */
export async function addManualFolder(context: vscode.ExtensionContext): Promise<string | null> {
  const result = await vscode.window.showOpenDialog({
    canSelectFolders: true,
    canSelectFiles: false,
    canSelectMany: false,
    openLabel: 'Ê∑ªÂäÝÁ¥ÝÊùêÂ∫ì',
  });

  if (!result || result.length === 0) return null;

  const folderPath = result[0].fsPath;
  const existing = getManualFolders(context);

  if (existing.includes(folderPath)) return null;

  existing.push(folderPath);
  await context.workspaceState.update('assetFolders', existing);
  return folderPath;
}

/** ÁßªÈô§ÊâãÂä®Á¥ÝÊùêÂ∫ì */
export async function removeManualFolder(context: vscode.ExtensionContext, folderPath: string) {
  const existing = getManualFolders(context);
  const idx = existing.indexOf(folderPath);
  if (idx >= 0) {
    existing.splice(idx, 1);
    await context.workspaceState.update('assetFolders', existing);
  }
}

/**
 * Â∞ÜÁ¥ÝÊùêÂ∫ìÂõæÁâáË∑ØÂæÑËΩ¨‰∏∫ÂèØÊèíÂÖ• Markdown ÁöÑÁõ∏ÂØπÂºïÁî®Â≠óÁ¨¶‰∏≤
 * Âê´Á©∫ÊÝº/Êã¨Âè∑Êó∂Áî®Â∞ñÊã¨Âè∑ÂåÖË£πÔºõË∑®ÁõòÁ¨¶ËøîÂõû null
 */
export function imageRelativeRef(
  imagePath: string,
  mdDir: string
): string | null {
  let rel: string;
  try {
    rel = path.posix.relative(mdDir, imagePath);
  } catch {
    return null;
  }

  if (path.isAbsolute(rel)) return null;

  rel = rel.replace(/\\/g, '/');

  if (!rel.startsWith('..') && !rel.startsWith('./')) {
    rel = './' + rel;
  }

  const needsBracket = /[\s()]/.test(rel);
  const displayPath = needsBracket ? `<${rel}>` : rel;

  const alt = path.basename(imagePath, path.extname(imagePath));

  return `![${alt}](${displayPath})`;
}

```typescript
const IMAGE_EXTENSIONS = ['png', 'jpg', 'jpeg', 'gif', 'webp', 'bmp', 'svg'];

export function isImageExt(ext: string): boolean {
  const cleaned = ext.replace(/^\./, '').toLowerCase();
  return IMAGE_EXTENSIONS.includes(cleaned);
}

export function extFromUrl(url: string): string {
  try {
    const pathname = new URL(url).pathname;
    const dotIdx = pathname.lastIndexOf('.');
    if (dotIdx === -1) return 'png';
    const ext = pathname.slice(dotIdx + 1).toLowerCase();
    return isImageExt(ext) ? ext : 'png';
  } catch {
    return 'png';
  }
}

const MIME_MAP: Record<string, string> = {
  png: 'image/png',
  jpg: 'image/jpeg',
  jpeg: 'image/jpeg',
  gif: 'image/gif',
  webp: 'image/webp',
  bmp: 'image/bmp',
  svg: 'image/svg+xml',
};

export function extToMime(ext: string): string {
  return MIME_MAP[ext.toLowerCase().replace(/^\./, '')] ?? 'image/png';
}

/** gpt-image-2-vip ÊØî‰æã+ÂàÜËæ®Áéá ‚Üí ÂÉèÁ¥ÝÂÄºÊç¢ÁÆó */
const VIP_PIXEL_TABLE: Record<string, Record<string, string | undefined> | undefined> = {
  '1:1': { '1K': '1024x1024', '2K': '2048x2048', '4K': '3072x3072' },
  '3:4': { '1K': '864x1152', '2K': '1728x2304', '4K': '2592x3456' },
  '4:3': { '1K': '1152x864', '2K': '2304x1728', '4K': '3456x2592' },
  '16:9': { '1K': '1280x720', '2K': '2560x1440', '4K': '3840x2160' },
  '9:16': { '1K': '720x1280', '2K': '1440x2560', '4K': '2160x3840' },
};

export function vipPixelValue(aspectRatio: string, imageSize: string): string {
  const row = VIP_PIXEL_TABLE[aspectRatio];
  if (!row) return '864x1152';
  const pixel = row[imageSize];
  return pixel || '864x1152';
}

/** Âà§Êñ≠Ê®°ÂûãÂ±û‰∫éÂì™‰∏™Á≥ªÂàó */
export type ModelFamily = 'nano-banana' | 'gpt-image-2' | 'gpt-image-2-vip';

export function modelFamily(model: string): ModelFamily {
  if (model.startsWith('gpt-image-2-vip')) return 'gpt-image-2-vip';
  if (model.startsWith('gpt-image-2')) return 'gpt-image-2';
  return 'nano-banana';
}

import * as assert from 'assert';
import * as path from 'path';
import * as fs from 'fs';
import * as os from 'os';
import { parseMarkdown } from './markdown-parser';

suite('markdown-parser', () => {
  let tmpDir: string;

  setup(() => {
    tmpDir = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'image-flow-test-'));
  });

  teardown(() => {
    fs.rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true });
  });

  function makeImg(name: string) {
    fs.writeFileSync(path.join(tmpDir, name), Buffer.from([0x89, 0x50, 0x4e, 0x47]));
  }

  test('ÊôÆÈÄöË∑ØÂæÑËß£Êûê', () => {
    makeImg('ref.png');
    const raw = 'ÁîüÊàê‰∏ÄÂºÝÂõæÔºåÂèÇËÄÉ ![alt](ref.png) È£éÊÝº';
    const { body, images, errors } = parseMarkdown(raw, tmpDir);
    assert.strictEqual(errors.length, 0);
    assert.strictEqual(images.length, 1);
    assert.strictEqual(images[0].index, 1);
    assert.strictEqual(images[0].path, 'ref.png');
    assert.ok(images[0].dataUri.startsWith('data:image/png;base64,'));
    assert.strictEqual(body, 'ÁîüÊàê‰∏ÄÂºÝÂõæÔºåÂèÇËÄÉ [image1](ref) È£éÊÝº');
  });

  test('Â∞ñÊã¨Âè∑Ë∑ØÂæÑÂê´Á©∫ÊÝº', () => {
    makeImg('my ref (1).png');
    const raw = 'ÊµãËØï ![desc](<my ref (1).png>) ÂõæÁâá';
    const { body, images, errors } = parseMarkdown(raw, tmpDir);
    assert.strictEqual(errors.length, 0);
    assert.strictEqual(images.length, 1);
    assert.strictEqual(images[0].path, 'my ref (1).png');
    assert.strictEqual(body, 'ÊµãËØï [image1](my ref (1)) ÂõæÁâá');
  });

  test('ÈáçÂ§çÂºïÁî®Â§çÁî®ÁºñÂè∑', () => {
    makeImg('a.png');
    const raw = '![x](a.png) ‰∏≠Èó¥ ![y](a.png) ÂÜçÁî® ![z](a.png)';
    const { body, images, errors } = parseMarkdown(raw, tmpDir);
    assert.strictEqual(errors.length, 0);
    assert.strictEqual(images.length, 1);
    assert.strictEqual(images[0].index, 1);
    assert.ok(body.includes('[image1](a)'));
    const matches = body.match(/\[image1\]/g);
    assert.strictEqual(matches?.length, 3);
  });

  test('Â§öÂõæÁâáÁºñÂè∑‰∏éÈ°∫Â∫è‰∏ÄËá¥', () => {
    makeImg('first.jpg');
    makeImg('second.gif');
    makeImg('third.webp');
    const raw = '![a](first.jpg) x ![b](second.gif) x ![c](third.webp)';
    const { body, images, errors } = parseMarkdown(raw, tmpDir);
    assert.strictEqual(errors.length, 0);
    assert.strictEqual(images.length, 3);
    assert.strictEqual(images[0].index, 1);
    assert.strictEqual(images[1].index, 2);
    assert.strictEqual(images[2].index, 3);
    assert.ok(body.includes('[image1](first)'));
    assert.ok(body.includes('[image2](second)'));
    assert.ok(body.includes('[image3](third)'));
  });

  test('ÂèÇËÄÉÂõæËØªÂèñÂ§±Ë¥•Êä•ÂëäÈîôËØØ', () => {
    const raw = '‰ΩøÁî®‰∏çÂ≠òÂú®ÂõæÁâá ![x](missing.png)';
    const { errors } = parseMarkdown(raw, tmpDir);
    assert.strictEqual(errors.length, 1);
    assert.ok(errors[0].endsWith('missing.png'));
  });

  test('Á©∫ÂÜÖÂÆπËøîÂõûÂéüÊÝ∑', () => {
    const { body, images, errors } = parseMarkdown('  ', tmpDir);
    assert.strictEqual(errors.length, 0);
    assert.strictEqual(images.length, 0);
    assert.strictEqual(body, '  ');
  });
});

suite('progress calculation', () => {
  function calcProgress(jobs: { status: string; progress: number }[]): number {
    const N = jobs.length;
    if (N === 0) return 0;
    let total = 0;
    for (const job of jobs) {
      if (job.status === 'succeeded' || job.status === 'failed' || job.status === 'violation') {
        total += 100;
      } else if (job.status === 'running') {
        total += job.progress;
      }
    }
    return Math.round(total / N);
  }

  test('ÂÖ® submitting ËÆ° 0', () => {
    const jobs = [{ status: 'submitting', progress: 0 }, { status: 'submitting', progress: 0 }];
    assert.strictEqual(calcProgress(jobs), 0);
  });

  test('Ê∑∑ÂêàÊÄÅ', () => {
    const jobs = [
      { status: 'succeeded', progress: 100 },
      { status: 'running', progress: 50 },
      { status: 'submitting', progress: 0 },
      { status: 'failed', progress: 0 },
    ];
    assert.strictEqual(calcProgress(jobs), 63);
  });

  test('ÂÖ®ÈÉ®ÁªàÁªì = 100', () => {
    const jobs = [{ status: 'succeeded', progress: 100 }, { status: 'failed', progress: 0 }];
    assert.strictEqual(calcProgress(jobs), 100);
  });
});

import * as assert from 'assert';
import { isTransientError, ApiError, submitGenerate, queryResult } from './api';

// --- mock fetch helper ---
let mockResponse: { status: number; body: unknown } | null = null;

function mockFetch(_url: string, _init?: RequestInit): Promise<Response> {
  if (!mockResponse) throw new Error('mockResponse not set');
  const { status, body } = mockResponse;
  return Promise.resolve({
    status,
    json: () => Promise.resolve(body),
  } as Response);
}

const originalFetch = globalThis.fetch;

suite('api - isTransientError', () => {
  test('5xx ‰∏∫Áû¨Êó∂ÈîôËØØ', () => {
    const err = new ApiError('ÊúçÂä°Âô®ÈîôËØØ 503', true);
    assert.strictEqual(isTransientError(err), true);
  });

  test('429 ‰∏∫Áû¨Êó∂ÈîôËØØ', () => {
    const err = new ApiError('ÈôêÊµÅ 429', true);
    assert.strictEqual(isTransientError(err), true);
  });

  test('ÊÝºÂºèÈîôËØØÈùûÁû¨Êó∂', () => {
    const err = new ApiError('ÂìçÂ∫îÊÝºÂºèÂºÇÂ∏∏', false);
    assert.strictEqual(isTransientError(err), false);
  });

  test('violation ÈùûÁû¨Êó∂', () => {
    const err = new ApiError('ËøùËßÑ', false);
    assert.strictEqual(isTransientError(err), false);
  });

  test('fetch failed ‰∏∫Áû¨Êó∂', () => {
    const err = new TypeError('fetch failed');
    assert.strictEqual(isTransientError(err), true);
  });

  test('abort ‰∏∫Áû¨Êó∂', () => {
    const abortErr = new Error('aborted');
    abortErr.name = 'AbortError';
    assert.strictEqual(isTransientError(abortErr), true);
  });
});

suite('api - runtime validation', () => {
  setup(() => {
    mockResponse = null;
    (globalThis as Record<string, unknown>).fetch = mockFetch;
  });

  teardown(() => {
    (globalThis as Record<string, unknown>).fetch = originalFetch;
  });

  function makeReq() {
    return {
      baseUrl: 'https://test.com', apiKey: 'sk-test', model: 'nano-banana-2',
      prompt: 'test', images: [] as string[], aspectRatio: '1:1', imageSize: '1K',
    };
  }

  test('submit null ÂìçÂ∫îÊäõÈùûÁû¨Êó∂ÈîôËØØ', async () => {
    mockResponse = { status: 200, body: null };
    try {
      await submitGenerate(makeReq());
      assert.fail('Â∫îËØ•ÊäõÈîô');
    } catch (err) {
      assert.ok(err instanceof ApiError);
      assert.strictEqual((err as ApiError).transient, false);
      assert.ok((err as ApiError).message.includes('ÊÝºÂºèÂºÇÂ∏∏'));
    }
  });

  test('submit Á©∫ id ÊäõÈùûÁû¨Êó∂ÈîôËØØ', async () => {
    mockResponse = { status: 200, body: { id: '', status: 'running' } };
    try {
      await submitGenerate(makeReq());
      assert.fail('Â∫îËØ•ÊäõÈîô');
    } catch (err) {
      assert.ok(err instanceof ApiError);
      assert.strictEqual((err as ApiError).transient, false);
    }
  });

  test('query Êú™Áü•Áä∂ÊÄÅÊäõÈùûÁû¨Êó∂ÈîôËØØ', async () => {
    mockResponse = { status: 200, body: { id: 'x', status: 'unknown_status' } };

      assert.strictEqual((err as ApiError).transient, false);
      assert.ok((err as ApiError).message.includes('Êú™Áü•Áä∂ÊÄÅ'));

  test('query 200 + ÊÝºÂºèÂºÇÂ∏∏ body ÊäõÈùûÁû¨Êó∂ÈîôËØØ', async () => {
    mockResponse = { status: 200, body: 'not json' };
    try {
      await queryResult('https://test.com', 'sk-test', 'job-1');
      assert.fail('Â∫îËØ•ÊäõÈîô');

});

```typescript
import * as assert from 'assert';
import * as path from 'path';
import * as fs from 'fs';
import * as os from 'os';
import { scanFolder, autoAssetFolders, imageRelativeRef } from './asset-library';

suite('asset-library', () => {
  let tmpDir: string;

  setup(() => {
    tmpDir = fs.mkdtempSync(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'image-flow-test-'));
  });

  teardown(() => {
    fs.rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true });
  });

  function makePng(name: string, subDir?: string) {
    const d = subDir ? path.join(tmpDir, subDir) : tmpDir;
    if (subDir) fs.mkdirSync(d, { recursive: true });
    fs.writeFileSync(path.join(d, name), Buffer.from([0x89, 0x50, 0x4e, 0x47]));
  }

  suite('scanFolder', () => {
    test('Êâ´ÊèèÂà∞ÂõæÁâá', () => {
      makePng('a.png');
      makePng('b.jpg');
      makePng('c.txt');
      const result = scanFolder(tmpDir);
      assert.strictEqual(result.length, 2);
    });

    test('Ê∑±Â∫¶‰∏çË∂ÖËøá 3', () => {
      makePng('d4.png', 'l1/l2/l3/l4');
      const result = scanFolder(tmpDir);
      // l4 Âú®Ê∑±Â∫¶ 4Ôºå‰∏çÂ∫îÂá∫Áé∞
      const names = result.map(p => path.basename(p));
      assert.ok(!names.includes('d4.png'));
    });
  });

  suite('autoAssetFolders', () => {
    test('ÈÄêÂ±ÇÂèëÁé∞Âê´ÂõæÁâáÁõÆÂΩï', () => {
      const ws = tmpDir;
      const sub1 = path.join(ws, 'sub1');
      const sub2 = path.join(sub1, 'sub2');
      fs.mkdirSync(sub2, { recursive: true });
      makePng('img.png', 'sub1');
      const mdPath = path.join(sub2, 'test.md');
      fs.writeFileSync(mdPath, 'x');

      const folders = autoAssetFolders(mdPath, ws);
      assert.strictEqual(folders.length, 1);
      assert.strictEqual(folders[0], sub1);
    });

    test('ÊóÝÂõæÁâáËøîÂõûÁ©∫', () => {
      const sub1 = path.join(tmpDir, 'sub1');
      fs.mkdirSync(sub1, { recursive: true });
      const mdPath = path.join(sub1, 'test.md');
      fs.writeFileSync(mdPath, 'x');

      const folders = autoAssetFolders(mdPath, tmpDir);
      assert.strictEqual(folders.length, 0);
    });
  });

  suite('imageRelativeRef', () => {
    test('ÊôÆÈÄöË∑ØÂæÑÁîüÊàêÂºïÁî®', () => {
      const ref = imageRelativeRef(
        path.join(tmpDir, 'img.png'),
        tmpDir
      );
      assert.strictEqual(ref, '![img](./img.png)');
    });

    test('Âê´Á©∫ÊÝºÁî®Â∞ñÊã¨Âè∑ÂåÖË£π', () => {
      const imgPath = path.join(tmpDir, 'my image.png');
      fs.writeFileSync(imgPath, '');
      const ref = imageRelativeRef(imgPath, tmpDir);
      assert.ok(ref!.includes('<./my image.png>'));
    });

    test('Âê´Êã¨Âè∑Áî®Â∞ñÊã¨Âè∑ÂåÖË£π', () => {
      const imgPath = path.join(tmpDir, 'img(1).png');
      fs.writeFileSync(imgPath, '');
      const ref = imageRelativeRef(imgPath, tmpDir);
      assert.ok(ref!.includes('<./img(1).png>'));
    });

    test('‰∏äÁ∫ßÁõÆÂΩïÂºïÁî®', () => {
      const subDir = path.join(tmpDir, 'sub');
      fs.mkdirSync(subDir, { recursive: true });
      const ref = imageRelativeRef(
        path.join(tmpDir, 'img.png'),
        subDir
      );
      assert.strictEqual(ref, '![img](../img.png)');
    });
  });
});

```typescript
import React, { useState, useEffect, useCallback } from 'react';
import { createRoot } from 'react-dom/client';
import { Workbench } from './components/Workbench';
import { Tasks } from './components/Tasks';
import { Settings } from './components/Settings';
import { vscodeApi } from './vscode';
import type { AppConfig, TaskCardInfo, AssetFolderInfo, BackendMessage } from '../src/shared';

type TabId = 'workbench' | 'tasks' | 'settings';

const App: React.FC = () => {
  const [activeTab, setActiveTab] = useState<TabId>('workbench');
  const [config, setConfig] = useState<AppConfig | null>(null);
  const [effectiveMd, setEffectiveMd] = useState('');
  const [tasks, setTasks] = useState<TaskCardInfo[]>([]);
  const [history, setHistory] = useState<TaskCardInfo[]>([]);
  const [assetFolders, setAssetFolders] = useState<AssetFolderInfo[]>([]);
  const [statusMsg, setStatusMsg] = useState('');

  useEffect(() => {
    const handler = (event: MessageEvent<BackendMessage>) => {
      const msg = event.data;
      switch (msg.type) {
        case 'initData':
          setConfig(msg.data.config);
          setEffectiveMd(msg.data.effectiveMd);
          setTasks(msg.data.tasks);
          break;
        case 'configUpdate':
          setConfig(prev => prev ? { ...prev, ...msg.config } : null);
          break;
        case 'effectiveMdChange':
          setEffectiveMd(msg.path);
          break;
        case 'taskUpdate':
          setTasks(msg.tasks);
          if (msg.history) setHistory(msg.history);
          if (msg.autoSwitch) setActiveTab('tasks');
          break;
        case 'assetUpdate':
          setAssetFolders(msg.folders);
          break;
        case 'error':
          setStatusMsg(msg.message);
          break;
        case 'info':
          setStatusMsg(msg.message);
          break;
      }
    };
    window.addEventListener('message', handler);
    vscodeApi.postMessage({ type: 'init' });
    return () => window.removeEventListener('message', handler);
  }, []);

  const sendConfig = useCallback((key: string, value: unknown) => {
    vscodeApi.postMessage({ type: 'setConfig', key, value });
  }, []);

  const mdFileName = effectiveMd
    ? effectiveMd.replace(/^.*[\\/]/, '')
    : 'Êú™ÊâìÂºÄ Markdown Êñá‰ª∂';

  return (
    <div className="app">
      <div className="tab-bar">
        <button className={activeTab === 'workbench' ? 'tab active' : 'tab'} onClick={() => setActiveTab('workbench')}>Â∑•‰ΩúÂè∞</button>
        <button className={activeTab === 'tasks' ? 'tab active' : 'tab'} onClick={() => setActiveTab('tasks')}>‰ªªÂä°</button>
        <button className={activeTab === 'settings' ? 'tab active' : 'tab'} onClick={() => setActiveTab('settings')}>ËÆæÁΩÆ</button>
      </div>

      <div className="tab-content" style={{ display: activeTab === 'workbench' ? 'block' : 'none' }}>
        {config && <Workbench config={config} onConfigChange={sendConfig} mdFileName={mdFileName} statusMsg={statusMsg} assetFolders={assetFolders} />}
      </div>
      <div className="tab-content" style={{ display: activeTab === 'tasks' ? 'block' : 'none' }}>
        <Tasks tasks={tasks} history={history} cols={config?.tasksCols ?? 2} />
      </div>
      <div className="tab-content" style={{ display: activeTab === 'settings' ? 'block' : 'none' }}>
        {config && <Settings config={config} onConfigChange={sendConfig} />}
      </div>
    </div>
  );
};

const root = createRoot(document.getElementById('root')!);
root.render(<App />);

```
</details>

```typescript
import React from 'react';
import { vscodeApi } from '../vscode';
import { AssetPanel } from './AssetPanel';
import type { AppConfig, AssetFolderInfo } from '../../src/shared';

interface Props {
  config: AppConfig;
  onConfigChange: (key: string, value: unknown) => void;
  mdFileName: string;
  statusMsg: string;
  assetFolders: AssetFolderInfo[];
}

export const Workbench: React.FC<Props> = ({ config, onConfigChange, mdFileName, statusMsg, assetFolders }) => {
  return (
    <div className="workbench">
      <AssetPanel
        folders={assetFolders}
        onInsert={(imagePath) => {
          vscodeApi.postMessage({ type: 'insertReference', imagePath });
        }}
        onAdd={() => vscodeApi.postMessage({ type: 'addAssetFolder' })}
        onRemove={(folderPath) => vscodeApi.postMessage({ type: 'removeAssetFolder', folderPath })}
        cols={config.workbenchCols}
      />

      <div className="controls-row">
        <select value={config.model} onChange={e => onConfigChange('model', e.target.value)}>
          <option value="nano-banana-2">nano-banana-2</option>
          <option value="nano-banana-pro">nano-banana-pro</option>
          <option value="gpt-image-2">gpt-image-2</option>
          <option value="gpt-image-2-vip">gpt-image-2-vip</option>
        </select>
        <select value={config.imageSize} onChange={e => onConfigChange('imageSize', e.target.value)}>
          <option value="1K">1K</option>
          <option value="2K">2K</option>
          <option value="4K">4K</option>
        </select>
        <select value={config.aspectRatio} onChange={e => onConfigChange('aspectRatio', e.target.value)}>
          <option value="1:1">1:1</option>
          <option value="3:4">3:4</option>
          <option value="4:3">4:3</option>
          <option value="16:9">16:9</option>
          <option value="9:16">9:16</option>
        </select>
        <div className="stepper">
          <label>Âπ∂Âèë: {config.concurrency}</label>
          <input type="range" min={1} max={10} value={config.concurrency}
            onChange={e => onConfigChange('concurrency', parseInt(e.target.value, 10))} />
        </div>
      </div>

      <div className="generate-row">
        <div className="current-md">{mdFileName}</div>
        <button onClick={() => vscodeApi.postMessage({ type: 'previewRequest', mdPath: '' })}>È¢ÑËßàËØ∑Ê±Ç</button>
        <button className="primary" onClick={() => vscodeApi.postMessage({ type: 'generate', mdPath: '' })}>ÁîüÊàê</button>
      </div>

      {statusMsg && <div className="status-line">{statusMsg}</div>}
    </div>
  );
};

```
</details>

import React from 'react';
import type { TaskCardInfo } from '../../src/shared';
import { TaskCard } from './TaskCard';

interface Props {
  tasks: TaskCardInfo[];
  history: TaskCardInfo[];
  cols: number;
}

export const Tasks: React.FC<Props> = ({ tasks, history, cols: _cols }) => {
  const all = [...tasks, ...history];
  if (all.length === 0) return (
    <div className="tasks-page">
      <h3>‰ªªÂä°</h3>
      <p className="empty-hint">ÊöÇÊóÝ‰ªªÂä°</p>
    </div>
  );

  return (
    <div className="tasks-page">
      <h3>‰ªªÂä°</h3>
      {all.map(t => (
        <TaskCard key={t.folderName} task={t} />
      ))}
    </div>
  );
};

```
</details>

```typescript
import * as path from 'path';
import { runTests } from '@vscode/test-electron';

async function main() {
  try {
    const extensionDevelopmentPath = path.resolve(__dirname, '../../');
    const extensionTestsPath = path.resolve(__dirname, '../');

    await runTests({
      extensionDevelopmentPath,
      extensionTestsPath,
      launchArgs: ['--disable-extensions'],
    });
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Failed to run tests:', err);
    process.exit(1);
  }
}

void main();

```
</details>

###### üëÄ Read `esbuild.config.mjs`

`02:16:53`

<details>
<summary><code>/home/particle/2026-1/harness-human-research/image-flow/runs/eval-manual-superpowers-260611093413/esbuild.config.mjs</code></summary>

```
import esbuild from 'esbuild';

const watch = process.argv.includes('--watch');

/** @type {esbuild.BuildOptions} */
const extensionConfig = {
  entryPoints: ['src/extension.ts'],
  bundle: true,
  platform: 'node',
  format: 'cjs',
  external: ['vscode'],
  outfile: 'dist/extension.js',
  sourcemap: true,
  logLevel: 'info',
};

/** @type {esbuild.BuildOptions} */
const sidebarConfig = {
  entryPoints: ['media/sidebar.tsx'],
  bundle: true,
  platform: 'browser',
  format: 'iife',
  outfile: 'media/sidebar.js',
  sourcemap: true,
  logLevel: 'info',
};

async function build() {
  const ctxExt = await esbuild.context(extensionConfig);
  const ctxSide = await esbuild.context(sidebarConfig);

  if (watch) {
    await ctxExt.watch();
    await ctxSide.watch();
    console.log('Watching for changes...');
  } else {
    await ctxExt.rebuild();
    await ctxExt.dispose();
    await ctxSide.rebuild();
    await ctxSide.dispose();
    console.log('Build complete.');
  }
}

build().catch(() => process.exit(1));

```
</details>

##### üîó Sub-assistant: *Now I have all the information needed for‚Ä¶*

`02:20:13`

<details>
<summary>Thinking...</summary>

> Now I have a comprehensive view of the entire codebase. Let me systematically analyze each area of the spec against the implementation. Let me organize my findings.
> 
> ## Analysis Categories
> 
> ### SPEC ¬ß4: Config System
> 
> **4.1 Config Items**
> - Implementation: `src/config.ts` readConfig/writeConfigField works with globalState for most items, secrets for apiKey. Package.json defines the configuration schema. ‚úì
> 
> **4.2 Storage**
> - apiKey in secrets: Yes, `writeConfigField` handles `apiKey` key and writes to `context.secrets.store('imageFlow.apiKey', ...)`. ‚úì
> - Other config in globalState: Yes. ‚úì
> - Asset folders in workspaceState: Yes, `getManualFolders`/`addManualFolder` use `context.workspaceState`. ‚úì
> - Patch merge: `writeConfigField` writes individual keys. ‚úì
> 
> **4.3 Model Injections**
> - Seeds: `seedModelInjections` seeds `gpt-image-2` and `gpt-image-2-vip` in `config.ts`. ‚úì
> - Only if key doesn't exist: `if (!(model in stored))`. ‚úì
> - Activation ordering: In `extension.ts`, `seedModelInjections` is called before `registerWebviewViewProvider`. ‚úì
> 
> ### SPEC ¬ß5: Markdown Parsing
> 
> **5. Image regex pattern** in `src/markdown-parser.ts`:
> ```js
> const IMAGE_RE = /!\[([^\]]*)\]\((<[^>]+>|[^)\s]+)\)/g;
> ```
> 
> **ISSUE**: The `|` branch for non-angle-bracket paths `[^)\s]+` doesn't handle paths with parentheses (e.g., `![alt](image(1).png)`). The spec says angle brackets can contain spaces and parentheses, but bare parentheses in non-angle-bracket paths won't be matched by `[^)\s]+`. However, maybe this is the intended behavior - you should use angle brackets for parens. Let me re-read the spec.
> 
> Spec ¬ß5 says: "Ë∑ØÂæÑÊîØÊåÅÂèØÈÄâÁöÑ `<>` ÂåÖË£πÂΩ¢Âºè `![alt](<Â∏¶ Á©∫ÊÝº(Êã¨Âè∑)ÁöÑË∑ØÂæÑ.png>)`‚Äî‚ÄîÂ∞ñÊã¨Âè∑ÂÜÖÂèØÂê´Á©∫ÊÝº‰∏éÂçäËßíÊã¨Âè∑„ÄÇ" So the spec allows angle bracket forms to contain parentheses. For non-angle-bracket paths, `[^)\s]+` would stop at a `)` character which is correct. This seems fine.
> 
> The regex also doesn't match paths that don't have an extension (just filenames without dots) - but that's fine for image paths.
> 
> Actually wait, I see another issue. The regex uses `[^)\s]+` for the non-angle-bracket case. This means:
> 1. It won't match paths with spaces (you need `<>` for that) ‚úì
> 2. It won't match paths with `)` in them but that's the correct behavior since `)` closes the markdown link syntax
> 
> But what about paths that look like `![alt](path/to/image.png)` with no special chars? That works. ‚úì
> 
> **Deduplication**: Uses `urlMap` with `cleanPath` as key. ‚úì
> 
> **Error handling**: Errors collected and returned but note in the spec: "‰ªª‰ΩïÂèÇËÄÉÂõæËØªÂèñÂ§±Ë¥•Ôºö‰∏≠Êñ≠ÁîüÊàêÔºåÊä•ÈîôÂπ∂ÂàóÂá∫ÂÖ®ÈÉ®Â§±Ë¥•Ë∑ØÂæÑ„ÄÇ" The current implementation continues processing all images even when some fail, collecting all errors. Then the caller (extension.ts or task-manager.ts) stops if errors.length > 0. This is actually better than the spec - "‰∏≠Êñ≠ÁîüÊàê" means "stop generation" which is what happens since the caller throws. ‚úì
> 
> **Empty content**: Checked in `extension.ts:55` and `task-manager.ts:79`. ‚úì
> 
> **Data URI conversion**: Yes, via `readFileSync` ‚Üí base64 with MIME from `extToMime`. ‚úì
> 
> **Body replacement**: `[imageN](filename_without_ext)`. ‚úì
> 
> ### SPEC ¬ß6: API Layer
> 
> **6. Size field handling** in `buildRequestBody` (api.ts):
> - nano-banana: aspectRatio + imageSize ‚úì
> - gpt-image-2: aspectRatio only ‚úì
> - gpt-image-2-vip: pixel value ‚úì
> 
> **Runtime validation**: `submitGenerate` checks for null/empty id. `queryResult` validates status enum. ‚úì
> 
> **Timeout**: 30s via `fetchWithTimeout`. ‚úì
> 
> **Transient errors**: 5xx/429, fetch failed, abort handled in `isTransientError`. ‚úì
> 
> ### SPEC ¬ß7: Task Lifecycle
> 
> **7.1 Submit**:
> - apiKey check: `task-manager.ts:74-76` ‚úì
> - Read MD ‚Üí parse ‚Üí assemble prompt: lines 78-89 ‚úì
> - Create task folder: line 91 (`createTaskFolder`) ‚úì
> - **Immediate card return**: lines 118-122 - task added to map, persisted, emitted BEFORE network calls. ‚úì
> - Background N requests: lines 126-128 with `void this.submitJob(...)`. ‚úì
> - All fail handling: `submitJob` lines 168-173 checks and calls `failTask`. ‚úì
> - Auto-switch to tasks tab: line 121, `emitUpdate(true)` passes autoSwitch=true. ‚úì
> 
> **7.2 Polling and Download**:
> - Single timer at 4s: `startPolling` uses `setInterval`. ‚úì
> - Reentrant lock: `polling` boolean flag (line 191). ‚úì
> - Download results: `downloadFile` called for each result URL. ‚úì
> - File naming: `{mdBaseName}-{seq}.{ext}` with seq being `task.downloadedImages.length + 1`. ‚úì
> - Extension from URL: `extFromUrl` with fallback to png. ‚úì
> - Failed/violation recording: Yes. ‚úì
> - Timeout (10 min): using `sessionStartTime`. ‚úì
> - Cleanup after all jobs done: Yes, `finishTask`. ‚úì
> - Notification on failures: warning vs error based on successCount. ‚úì
> 
> **7.3 Prompt Injection**:
> - Implementation in `prompt-injection.ts`:
>   - Model injection + IMAGES.md + body joined with '\n\n'
>   - Empty segments omitted ‚úì
>   - IMAGES.md non-existence handled with try/catch ‚úì
> 
> **7.4 Persistence and Resume**:
> - globalState storage: `persist()` writes to `STORAGE_KEY`. ‚úì
> - Resume in `activate()`: `taskManager.resume()` called. ‚úì
> - Timeout from session start: `sessionStartTime` reset on resume. ‚úì

The `_editorListener` is set up in `resolveWebviewView`. So if the user has a .md file open, then opens the sidebar, the editor listener fires and sets `_effectiveMd`. But there's an edge case: what if the `init` message fires before the `onDidChangeActiveTextEditor` event? Let me check...

> In `resolveWebviewView` (sidebar-provider.ts:35-58):
> 1. Store view reference
> 2. Set webview options
> 3. Set HTML
> 4. Register message handler (which handles `init`)
> 5. Register editor listener
> 
> When the webview loads, it immediately sends `init`. But the editor listener fires asynchronously. So on first load:
> - `Init` message handler ‚Üí `sendInitData` ‚Üí sends `effectiveMd: this._effectiveMd` (which is '' because the listener hasn't fired yet)
> - Later, the editor listener fires ‚Üí sets `this._effectiveMd` and sends `effectiveMdChange`
>

> ### Deep bugs and issues
> 
> Now let me look more carefully for race conditions, memory leaks, and security issues.
> 
> #### Race Conditions
> 
> **task-manager.ts - submit() race:**
> ```js
> // Line 118-122: Task added, persisted, emitted
> this.tasks.set(folderName, task);
> this.persist();
> this.emitUpdate(true);
> this.startPolling();
> 
> // Line 126-128: Background jobs start
> for (const job of jobs) {
>   void this.submitJob(task, job, finalPrompt, refImages, config);
> }
> ```
> 
> The race is between:
> - `pollAll()` (which checks for running jobs) 
> - `submitJob()` (which transitions jobs from 'submitting' to 'running')
> 
> But this is not really a race since:
> 1. Task is added to map before `startPolling`
> 2. `pollAll` checks `job.status === 'running'` and `job.status === 'submitting'` - submitting jobs are just left alone (not queried)
> 3. Once `submitJob` completes (transitions to running), the next poll will pick it up
> 
> This seems safe.
> 
> **task-manager.ts - submitJob() retry race:**
> ```js
> if (isTransientError(err) && job.transientRetries < MAX_RETRY) {
>   job.transientRetries++;
>   this.persist();
>   await this.submitJob(task, job, prompt, refImages, config);
>   return;  // <-- This return is important
> }
> ```
> The recursive call with `return` prevents the "allDone" check below from running early. ‚úì
> 
> **pollAll() reentrant lock:**
> ```js
> if (this.polling) return;
> this.polling = true;
> try {
>   // ...
> } finally {
>   this.polling = false;
> }
> ```
> ‚úì This prevents overlapping polls.
> 
> **BUT**: There's a timing issue between `submitJob` and `pollAll`. After `startPolling()` is called, the interval is 4s. If `pollAll` runs before `submitJob` transitions a job to 'running', the job stays in 'submitting' state. `pollAll` only queries jobs in 'running' state (line 213). 'submitting' jobs are checked for `allDone` (line 245-247). So if all jobs are still 'submitting', `allDone` would be false, which is correct. ‚úì
> 
> **Memory leak - event listener cleanup:**
> - `sidebar-provider.ts:50-57`: `this._editorListener` is set in `resolveWebviewView` and disposed in `dispose()`. The `resolveWebviewView` can be called multiple times if the webview is recreated. Each time, a new listener is created without disposing the old one. But wait, `resolveWebviewView` sets `this._view = webviewView` and creates a new listener. The old `_editorListener` is never disposed before creating a new one.
> 
> **CRITICAL MEMORY LEAK** in `TaskCard.tsx:22`:
> ```jsx
> const timer = setInterval(update, 1000);
> return () => clearInterval(timer);
> ```
> This is cleaned up on unmount or dependency change. ‚úì
> 
> **CRITICAL MEMORY LEAK** in sidebar.tsx:50-54:
> ```jsx
> window.addEventListener('message', handler);
> ...
> return () => window.removeEventListener('message', handler);
> ```
> This is a React useEffect cleanup. ‚úì
> 
> #### Incorrect State Management
> 
> **sidebar-provider.ts: `resolveWebviewView` can be called multiple times:**
> VS Code can call `resolveWebviewView` again if the webview is hidden and reshown. Each call:
> 1. Sets a new `_view` reference
> 2. Sets webview options
> 3. Sets HTML
> 4. Registers a new message handler
> 5. Registers a new editor listener - **without disposing the old one**
> 
> This is both a memory leak AND could cause the old handler to run on a disposed webview. The `onDidChangeActiveTextEditor` listener is reassigned without `.dispose()` on the old one.
> 
> Actually wait - would VS Code call `resolveWebviewView` multiple times for the same view? In the VS Code API docs, for `registerWebviewViewProvider`, `resolveWebviewView` is called each time the view becomes visible. If it was hidden and reshown, it'd be called again.
> 
> This means:
> - The old `_editorListener` is leaked (no `.dispose()`)
> - The old webview's message handlers might continue running
> 
> But actually, when the old webview is disposed, does VS Code clean up the `onDidReceiveMessage` listener? I believe VS Code handles this properly - when a webview is disposed, its listeners are cleaned up. But the `_editorListener` is on `vscode.window.onDidChangeActiveTextEditor`, which is global and won't be auto-cleaned.
> 
> **BUG**: In `sidebar-provider.ts:50`, each call to `resolveWebviewView` creates a new `_editorListener` without disposing the previous one.
> 
> The `_editorListener` is a class property initialized as `undefined`, and `dispose()` only calls `this._editorListener?.dispose()`. But `resolveWebviewView` doesn't check for existing listener before reassigning.
> 
> **bug in `scanHistory()` - path joining issue:**
> Looking at line 245:
> ```
> downloadedImages: imageFiles.map(f => path.join(taskDir, f)),
> ```
> These are absolute file paths to the image files. But since the webview needs `asWebviewUri`, and there's no conversion, this is effectively broken.
> 
> Also, the `scanHistory` function returns `TaskCardInfo[]` with `startTime: 0` which is actually fine for history cards (the elapsed timer won't show for history because `task.isActive` is false).
> 
> Actually wait - I need to check something. In the `taskUpdate` message, the sidebar-provider sends `history` but look at the `taskUpdate` handler in `sidebar.tsx`:
> ```
> case 'taskUpdate':
>   setTasks(msg.tasks);
>   if (msg.history) setHistory(msg.history);
>   if (msg.autoSwitch) setActiveTab('tasks');
>   break;
> ```
> This works. But now look at `scanHistory` - it returns `TaskCardInfo[]` with `folderName` being the directory name (like `task-...`) and `downloadedImages` being absolute paths. These are used in `Tasks.tsx` and rendered by `TaskCard.tsx`. The image src will be an absolute filesystem path, which won't work in the webview.
> 
> #### Edge Cases Not Handled
> 
> **`downloadFile` timeout**: In `task-manager.ts:377-382`:
> ```js
> async function downloadFile(url: string, dest: string): Promise<void> {
>   const res = await fetch(url);
>   if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`‰∏ãËΩΩÂ§±Ë¥•: ${res.status}`);
>   const buf = Buffer.from(await res.arrayBuffer());
>   fs.writeFileSync(dest, buf);
> }
> ```
> **BUG**: `downloadFile` has NO timeout. If the image URL is slow or the server hangs, this can block pollAll indefinitely (since it's awaited in the poll loop at line 225). The spec says "ÊâÄÊúâÁΩëÁªúËØ∑Ê±ÇÂ∏¶Ë∂ÖÊó∂Ôºà30s ÈáèÁ∫ßÔºâ" but `downloadFile` doesn't respect this.
> 
> Also, `downloadFile` is called inside `pollAll` which has the reentrant lock. If a download hangs, the reentrant lock prevents any subsequent polls. This is a **CRITICAL BUG**.
> 
> **MIME type validation**: In `markdown-parser.ts:38`, `extToMime` returns `image/png` as fallback for unknown extensions. This means a user could reference a non-image file (e.g., `ref.txt`) and it would try to include it as a `data:image/png;base64,...` in the API request. However, the API might reject it. This is a minor issue.
> 
> Actually, re-checking: the markdown parser only processes `![alt](path)` syntax, and typing `![alt](text.txt)` would be unusual. Also, the `extToMime` function uses the whitelist internally and only falls back to png. Let me re-check...
> 
> From `image-utils.ts:30-32`:
> ```js
> export function extToMime(ext: string): string {
>   return MIME_MAP[ext.toLowerCase().replace(/^\./, '')] ?? 'image/png';
> }
> ```
> 
> So for an unknown extension, it returns `image/png`. The spec requires the whitelist to be used for "Á¥ÝÊùêÂ∫ìÊâ´Êèè„ÄÅÂéÜÂè≤Êâ´Êèè„ÄÅ‰∏ãËΩΩÊâ©Â±ïÂêçÊÝ°È™å‰∏é mime Êé®Êñ≠ÂÖ±Áî®ÁöÑÁôΩÂêçÂçï". The `extToMime` function should return a known mime or reject, not fall back to `image/png` for non-image files. But in practice, this would only be triggered by non-image files referenced in markdown, which is already handled by `isImageExt` in other places. The markdown parser itself doesn't validate that the reference is an image file.
> 
> **MINOR issue**: The markdown parser doesn't validate that the referenced file is actually an image (by extension). It tries to read anything referenced in `![alt](path)` syntax and create a data URI from it with a possibly incorrect MIME type.
> 
> **Concurrency limit validation**: In `task-manager.ts:94`:
> ```js
> const N = Math.min(config.concurrency, 10);
> ```
> This ensures concurrency is capped at 10, but `config.concurrency` could theoretically be less than 1 (though the VS Code config system should enforce min=1). If it were 0, we'd create 0 jobs and have an empty task. But this is guarded by VS Code's configuration constraints.
> 
> **`createTaskFolder` unique naming**: Looking at `task-manager.ts:364-375`:
> ```js
> function createTaskFolder(mdDir: string): string {
>   const now = new Date();
>   const ts = `${String(now.getFullYear()).slice(2)}${pad(now.getMonth() + 1)}${pad(now.getDate())}${pad(now.getHours())}${pad(now.getMinutes())}${pad(now.getSeconds())}`;
>   let seq = 0;
>   let name: string;
>   do {
>     seq++;
>     name = `task-${ts}-${seq}`;
>   } while (fs.existsSync(path.join(mdDir, name)));
>   fs.mkdirSync(path.join(mdDir, name));
>   return name;
> }
> ```
> 
> **POTENTIAL RACE CONDITION**: Between the `fs.existsSync` check and `fs.mkdirSync`, another concurrent operation could create the same folder. Since JS is single-threaded and this is synchronous code, this is not actually a race in practice. However, if multiple submits happen in rapid succession within the same millisecond, they'd get different `seq` values since `seq` starts from 0 each time and increments until unique. ‚úì
> 
> #### Security Issues
> 
> **apiKey storage**: Stored in VS Code secrets (`context.secrets.store`). ‚úì No plain-text in globalState.
> 
> **CSP**: The CSP in `sidebar-provider.ts:87-90`:
> ```
> default-src 'none';
> img-src ${webview.cspSource} data: https:;
> style-src ${webview.cspSource} 'unsafe-inline';
> script-src 'nonce-${nonce}';
> ```
> 
> Issue: `img-src` includes `https:` which is very permissive. While webview CSP is informational (not strictly enforced in VS Code), it's still a best practice. The `https:` allows loading from any HTTPS source, which is probably intentional for image URLs. Not a major security concern since the webview loads user-controlled content anyway.
> 
> **localResourceRoots**: In `sidebar-provider.ts:60-71`:
> ```js
> private buildLocalResourceRoots(): vscode.Uri[] {
>   const roots = [
>     vscode.Uri.joinPath(this._extensionUri, 'media'),
>   ];
>   const workspaceFolders = vscode.workspace.workspaceFolders;
>   if (workspaceFolders) {
>     for (const folder of workspaceFolders) {
>       roots.push(folder.uri);
>     }
>   }
>   return roots;
> }
> ```
> 
> **BUG**: `localResourceRoots` includes the entire workspace folder, but the spec says "ÂåÖÂê´Êâ©Â±ï media ÁõÆÂΩï„ÄÅÂ∑•‰ΩúÂå∫ÁõÆÂΩï‰∏éÂÖ®ÈÉ®Á¥ÝÊùêÂ∫ìÁõÆÂΩïÔºåÁ¥ÝÊùêÂ∫ìÂ¢ûÂàÝÂêéÈúÄÈáçËÆæ" (should also contain all asset library directories, and should be reset when asset libraries are added/removed).
> 
> The current implementation:
> 1. Only includes workspace root, not individual asset library folders
> 2. Never updates when asset libraries change (no mechanism to rebuild and apply to the webview)
> 
> However, since the workspace root already encompasses all subdirectories (including asset library folders), this is practically sufficient for loading images. The spec requirement about "Á¥ÝÊùêÂ∫ìÂ¢ûÂàÝÂêéÈúÄÈáçËÆæ" (reset after asset library add/remove) is not explicitly implemented, but since all workspace sub-folders are included, it's functionally fine.
> 
> Actually, looking at the code again, `localResourceRoots` is set once in `resolveWebviewView` and never updated. The spec says it should be updated when asset libraries change. Since asset libraries can be outside the workspace (selected via folder picker), they might NOT be under the workspace root. This means images from external asset libraries won't be loadable in the webview.
> 
> **BUG**: Asset library folders outside the workspace root are not included in `localResourceRoots`. If a user adds a manual asset folder outside the workspace, images from that folder can be scanned and their `asWebviewUri` paths will be generated, but the webview won't be able to load them because the folder isn't in `localResourceRoots`.
> 
> Also, there's no mechanism to update `localResourceRoots` after the webview is created - `webviewView.webview.options` can be updated but there's no code to do that.
> 
> #### Type Safety Issues
> 
> **`shared.ts`**: The type definitions look correct. But there's an issue:
> 
> The `BackendMessage.taskUpdate` has an optional `history` field of type `TaskCardInfo[]`, but the `FrontendMessage` doesn't include a close variant. Let me check if there's a missing message type.
> 
> **`sidebar-provider.ts:206`**: `postMessage` just calls `webview.postMessage(msg)` which is typed as `void`. The return type is `Thenable<boolean>` but it's not awaited or checked. This is normal for VS Code API.
> 
> **`api.ts`**: The cast `data as Record<string, unknown>` at line 109 is fine because it's guarded by the null/object check above.
> 
> **`api.ts:166`**: `d.results as { url: string }[]` - This casts without validation. If `results` exists but has the wrong shape (e.g., `[{url: 123}]`), it would silently pass through. A runtime check for `typeof item.url === 'string'` would be safer.
> 
> **`sidebar-provider.ts`**: `buildAssetFolders` returns `AssetFolderInfo[]` where `images` has `url: string` from `webview.asWebviewUri(...).toString()` and `path: string` - the raw filesystem path. In the frontend, `AssetPanel.tsx` passes `img.path` for the `onContextMenu` and `onInsert` callbacks, which is the raw path. This goes through `handleInsertReference` which calls `imageRelativeRef` with the raw path. This is correct - the image path, not the webview URI, should be used for the reference. ‚úì
> 
> #### Other Bugs
> 
> **`handleInsertReference` checks for `.md` extension but not `_effectiveMd`:**
> ```js
> private async handleInsertReference(imagePath: string) {
>   const editor = vscode.window.activeTextEditor;
>   if (!editor) return;
>   const docUri = editor.document.uri;
>   if (!docUri.fsPath.endsWith('.md')) return;
>   ...
> }
> ```

> The spec says: "‰ªÖÂΩìÂΩìÂâçÊ¥ªÂä®ÁºñËæëÂô®Ê≠£ÊòØÁîüÊïà MD Êó∂ÊâçÊèíÂÖ•ÔºåÂê¶ÂàôË≠¶ÂëäÂπ∂ÂøΩÁï•ÔºàÈò≤ÊèíÈîôÊñá‰ª∂Ôºâ". The check should be `docUri.fsPath === this._effectiveMd`, not just `.endsWith('.md')`. Also missing is the warning message when it doesn't match.

> **BUG**: Spec gap - insert reference doesn't verify against `_effectiveMd`, and doesn't show warning on mismatch.
> 
> **`buildRequestBody` in extension.ts for preview**: In `extension.ts:69-77`:

> const requestBody = buildRequestBody({

> });
> ```
> This calls `buildRequestBody` which builds the same body as the real API call. Then in `showPreview`, the body is passed to `buildPreviewContent` which deep-copies and strips prompt/images. But the API key is passed as part of the request setup, not the body. ‚úì - Actually, `buildRequestBody` doesn't include apiKey in the body (it's in the header), so this is fine. ‚úì
> 
> **`Workbench.tsx` - `generate` and `previewRequest` send empty `mdPath`:**

> <button className="primary" onClick={() => vscodeApi.postMessage({ type: 'generate', mdPath: '' })}>ÁîüÊàê</button>
> ```
> 
> These send an empty `mdPath` string. In `sidebar-provider.ts`, the `handleMessage` method doesn't handle `generate` and `previewRequest` message types directly - it's not in the switch statement!
> 
> **CRITICAL BUG**: Looking at `sidebar-provider.ts:100-137`, the `handleMessage` switch includes:

> - `addAssetFolder`

> 
> This is a critical missing feature. The command handlers in `extension.ts` only handle the right-click menu commands, not the sidebar button clicks.
>

> 
> Looking at the frontend `Workbench.tsx`:
> ```jsx

> ```
> and
> ```jsx

> ```
> 
> These send messages directly. But in `sidebar-provider.ts`, the `handleMessage` switch has a `default: break;` which silently drops unhandled messages. `generate` and `previewRequest` fall through to `default`.

> **Workbench.tsx - Generate button not disabled during submission:**
> The spec says "ÁîüÊàêÊåâÈíÆÔºàÊèê‰∫§ÊúüÈó¥Á¶ÅÁî®Âπ∂ÊòæÁ§∫„ÄåÁîüÊàê‰∏≠‚Ä¶„ÄçÔºâ". The current implementation just has `<button className="primary" onClick={...}>ÁîüÊàê</button>`. No disabled state based on submitting status.
> 
> Actually, looking more carefully at the frontend-backend protocol:
> - The frontend sends `{ type: 'generate', mdPath: '' }` to the extension
> - Even if the handler existed, it'd need to use `this._effectiveMd` instead of `mdPath` (since it's empty)
> - The extension would need to call `taskManager.submit(vscode.Uri.file(this._effectiveMd))`
> 
> So the fix would be to handle `generate` message by reading `this._effectiveMd` and calling `taskManager.submit`.

> ```

Looking at ¬ß8.3: "ÁîüÊàêË°åÔºöÊòæÁ§∫ÂΩìÂâçÁîüÊïà MD Êñá‰ª∂ÂêçÔºàÊóÝÂàô„ÄåÊú™ÊâìÂºÄ Markdown Êñá‰ª∂„ÄçÔºâ+„ÄåÈ¢ÑËßàËØ∑Ê±Ç„ÄçÊåâÈíÆ +„ÄåÁîüÊàê„ÄçÊåâÈíÆÔºàÊèê‰∫§ÊúüÈó¥Á¶ÅÁî®Âπ∂ÊòæÁ§∫„ÄåÁîüÊàê‰∏≠‚Ä¶„ÄçÔºâ"

> #### Build/Config Issues
> 
> **`package.json` scripts:**
> - `compile`: `node esbuild.config.mjs` - but the spec says `compile` should run `check-types` and `lint` before building. The `compile` script doesn't include these. The `package` script does (`"package": "npm run check-types && npm run lint && npm run compile"`). So `compile` alone skips type checking and linting. The spec says:
>   - "npm ËÑöÊú¨Ëá≥Â∞ëÂåÖÂê´Ôºö`compile`„ÄÅ`watch`„ÄÅ`package`„ÄÅ`check-types`„ÄÅ`lint`„ÄÅ`test`"
>   - "ESLint flat configÔºå`compile` / `package` ËÑöÊú¨ÂâçÁΩÆÊâßË°å `check-types` ‰∏é `lint`"
>   
>   So the spec requires `compile` to pre-execute `check-types` and `lint`, but the current implementation's `compile` doesn't. This is a **spec gap**.
> 
> **Launch.json**: Has both "Run Extension" and "Extension Tests" configurations with `outFiles` pointing to `dist/` and `out/` respectively ‚úì. Includes `preLaunchTask: "npm: compile"` ‚úì.
> 
> #### Summary of Issues
> 
> Let me organize these by priority.
> 
> **CRITICAL:**
> 1. Webview image loading broken - task/history thumbnails are raw filesystem paths, not asWebviewUri
> 2. Sidebar generate/preview buttons do nothing - messages not handled in sidebar-provider.ts
> 3. downloadFile has no timeout, can block pollAll reentrant lock indefinitely
> 4. localResourceRoots doesn't include external asset library folders
> 
> **IMPORTANT:**
> 5. Generate button not disabled during submission (spec ¬ß8.3)
> 6. Task/history cards not collapsible (spec ¬ß8.4)
> 7. Thumbnail click doesn't open in VS Code (spec ¬ß8.4)
> 8. tasksCols not applied to task grid (spec ¬ß8.4)
> 9. Insert reference doesn't verify against _effectiveMd (spec ¬ß10.3)
> 10. Editor listener leak in resolveWebviewView (multiple calls)
> 11. gpt-image-2-vip pixel conversion not tested (spec ¬ß12)
> 12. `compile` script doesn't run check-types/lint first (spec ¬ß2)
> 13. Missing error notification for handleInsertReference mismatch
> 
> **MINOR:**
> 14. Markdown parser doesn't validate referenced file is actually an image
> 15. Preview request in extension.ts could share more code with submit
> 16. `extToMime` unconditionally falls back to `image/png` for non-image extensions
> 17. Results shape not fully validated (results items' url shape cast)
> 18. autoAssetFolders has an odd break condition with `path.dirname(workspaceRoot)` which doesn't make much sense
